sn5.6:1.1: At Sāvatthī. Then, in the morning, the bhikkhuni Cala dressed … she sat down at the foot of a tree for the day's abiding.
sn5.6:1.2: Then Māra the Evil One approached the bhikkhuni Cala and said to her: "What don’t you approve of, bhikkhuni?"
sn5.6:2.1: v.536"Why don’t you approve of birth?Once born, one enjoys sensual pleasures.Who now has persuaded you of this:‘Bhikkhunī, don’t approve of birth’?"
sn5.6:3.1: v.537"For one who is born there is death;Once born, one encounters sufferings—Bondage, murder, affliction—Hence one shouldn’t approve of birth.
sn5.6:4.1: v.538"The Buddha has taught the Dhamma,The transcendence of birth;For the abandoning of all sufferingHe has settled me in the truth.sn.i.133
sn5.6:5.1: v.539"As to those beings who fare amidst form,And those who abide in the formless—Not having understood cessation,They come again to renewed existence."
sn5.6:6.1: Then Māra the Evil One, realizing, "The bhikkhuni Cala knows me," sad and disappointed, disappeared right there.
